perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate the Agreement on 60-days written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment cease 30 days after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate the Agreement on 60-days written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment shall continue indefinitely after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the duration of MediaNet Group Technologies' payment obligation to the Reseller after termination of the agreement. Originally, payments cease 30 days after termination. The modified text states that the obligation to remit payment continues 'indefinitely'. This creates a contradiction in Section 9.1 as to how long MediaNet is obligated to pay Reseller after the agreement ends, thereby creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of 1 year after the Effective Date, unless earlier terminated as set forth herein (the "TERM"). This Agreement shall renew for successive 1-year periods, after the initial 1 Year Term, if agreed by both parties in writing within 30 days of license expiration. Either party may terminate the Agreement on 60-days written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment shall continue indefinitely after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of payment obligation after the agreement ends from '30 days after termination' to 'indefinitely'. This directly contradicts the original term, creating a strong inconsistency that would cause confusion and potential legal disputes. This inconsistency clearly violates Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The purpose of this Agreement is to define the scope of compensation for the marketing of Portals to these potential customers. During the Term of this Agreement, Reseller shall have the right to market Brand-A-Port Portals, both through the existing Brand-A-Port Portal under the License and independently to Buyers for resale, subject to approval by MediaNet Group Technologies.
perturbation.changed_text: The purpose of this Agreement is to define the scope of compensation for the marketing of Portals to these potential customers. During the Term of this Agreement, Reseller shall have the right to market Brand-A-Port Portals, both through the existing Brand-A-Port Portal under the License and independently to Buyers for resale, without any approval by MediaNet Group Technologies.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing MediaNet Group Technologies' right to approve the Reseller's independent marketing efforts of Brand-A-Port Portals. The original text requires MediaNet Group Technologies approval for Reseller's marketing. The modified text removes that requirement, creating a contradiction in Section 2 as to whether the Reseller needs approval or not. This leads to uncertainty about the scope of Reseller's marketing authority and creates potential for disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESELLING The Reseller intends to market the Portals to companies and various businesses and individuals ("BUYERS") who may include corporations, small businesses, religious organizations, network marketing, franchise, business opportunities, chains, charities, organizations and other companies that would have a use for individual and multiple portals. The purpose of this Agreement is to define the scope of compensation for the marketing of Portals to these potential customers. During the Term of this Agreement, Reseller shall have the right to market Brand-A-Port Portals, both through the existing Brand-A-Port Portal under the License and independently to Buyers for resale, without any approval by MediaNet Group Technologies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for MediaNet Group Technologies' approval for the Reseller's independent marketing efforts, directly contradicting the original requirement. The contradicted text includes the changed text, further solidifying the contradiction because it now states that the Reseller can market Brand-A-Port Portals independently without approval. This creates an inconsistency because it's unclear whether the Reseller requires MediaNet Group Technologies' approval to market Brand-A-Port Portals independently, leading to a potential dispute over the scope of Reseller's marketing authority, meeting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Resellers are expected to actively market and promote MediaNet Group Technologies Software to their existing customer base and visitors to their web site. Promotion by newsletters and e-mail to customers is encouraged, but we do not encourage spam e-mail and hence specifically ask resellers not to promote MediaNet Group Technologies Software or Applications via unsolicited e-mail.
perturbation.changed_text: Resellers are expected to actively market and promote MediaNet Group Technologies Software to their existing customer base and visitors to their web site. Promotion by newsletters and e-mail to customers is required, including the use of spam e-mail, to ensure maximum outreach.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by mandating the use of 'spam e-mail' for promotion, while the original text specifically discourages 'unsolicited e-mail'. This change creates a direct conflict within the Reselling Schedule, regarding the acceptable methods of promotion. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about the guidelines Resellers are expected to follow, which may create legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LICENSE RESELLING SCHEDULE Resellers are licensed to market the Brand-A-Port applications and receive commission on all product sales plus residual income on hosting/maintenance fees as outlined on the Compensation Schedule attached hereto. THE PLAN The MediaNet Group Technologies Reseller Plan is free to join. Under the plan, resellers market and promote MediaNet Group Technologies's software product range to their existing customer base and the general Internet community. By referring a customer to the MediaNet Group Technologies web site, the reseller receives a commission for customer's purchase of the MediaNet Group Technologies Brand-A-Port software. MARKETING AND PROMOTION Resellers are expected to actively market and promote MediaNet Group Technologies Software to their existing customer base and visitors to their web site. Promotion by newsletters and e-mail to customers is required, including the use of spam e-mail, to ensure maximum outreach. RESELLER LINK Customers are identified as coming from a particular reseller by placing a link(s) on their web site, which contains a cookie (i.e.. small packet of code) which identifies the reseller. On acceptance into the program, MediaNet Group Technologies issues the reseller with the cookie and instructions on how to insert the cookie into their web site. WHAT PRODUCTS ARE AVAILABLE FOR RESALE? Products which can be resold, include: Brand-A-Port Gold (Generic Portal), Brand-A-Port Platinum Portal and Branded PictureJudge applications. MediaNet Group Technologies may offer additional products from time to time. COMMISSION MediaNet Group Technologies tracks the customer sales coming from the reseller and at the end of every calendar month issues a check for 20% commission to the reseller on product sales and 10% the total amount of hosting/maintenance sales made. Commissions are only paid each month if the amount owing exceeds $100 US. MediaNet Group Technologies reserves the right to change the level of commission with out notice. Commission is not paid on MediaNet Group Technologies's free-ware programs. (pricing and products subject to change without notice). CUSTOMER CONTACT Once a customer has come from the reseller to the MediaNet Group Technologies web site, MediaNet Group Technologies deals with the customer directly, issuing the software from the MediaNet Group Technologies server and billing the customer. MediaNet Group Technologies handles all technical support and refund issues. REFUNDS If a refund is issued to a customer who came from a reseller, the reseller's account is adjusted accordingly. TERMINATION The Reseller Agreement can be terminated at any time at the discretion of either party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intention and wording of the Reseller Agreement by mandating the use of 'spam e-mail' for promotion. The original text specifically discourages 'unsolicited e-mail'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding acceptable promotion methods, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable as resellers are given conflicting instructions within the 'LICENSE RESELLING SCHEDULE' text.
